_comment: Machine-readable companion to py-resolution.md. The py_accuracy_gate report_matches_committed_doc test asserts these numbers equal the live differential (resolve_differential_graph over the committed Python corpus), so the published report cannot silently drift. Regenerate the figures with: cargo test -p strata-index --test py_accuracy_gate -- --nocapture print_py_corpus_report. Regenerate the committed index.scip with: STRATA_SCIP_LIVE=1 cargo test -p strata-index --test gen_scip -- --ignored generate_py
_by_band_comment: Per-confidence-band calibration (design §4.1): does an edge in band B resolve correctly at the rate B claims? precision is edge-level (confirmed/(confirmed+denied)) over SCIP-adjudicable edges; null = undefined (no adjudicable edges, never a vacuous 1.0). unadjudicable = sites in the band SCIP cannot ground-truth (e.g. an untyped-receiver fan-out scip-python cannot resolve, a constructor/import the heuristic emits no edge for), excluded from precision. RESOLVED carries no heuristic edge (the heuristic never claims compiler/SCIP grade). Unlike TS, the Python linker DOES emit EXTRACTED-band edges (a same-module bare call to a local def is a deterministic fact).
